ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-20-2012 Continued To Jul-18-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to Attorney Steven Weinkauf to appear on July 18, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to respond to the issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes. The accounting petition is also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Andrade.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-25-2012 Continued To May-23-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Weinkauf requested a continuance stating that he needs to file an amended petition, that he has had positive results with the Federal Estate Tax and that he does not think the Estate is going to be insolvent. The accounting petition was continued to May 23, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosada.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Nov-29-2011 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Petition For Final Distribution Or Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Oct-25-2011 Continued To Nov-29-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marie Bender-Guel in Propria Persona. Veronica Chico in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that Attorney Steven Weinkauf was not present. Ms. Bender-Guel and Ms. Chico expressed their concerns. An Order to Appear will be issued to Attorney Steven WEinkauf to appear on Novmeber 29, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the Petition for Final Distribution has not been filed. The matter was continued to November 11, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Petition For Final Distribution Or Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Sep-06-2011 Continued To Oct-25-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA) (Administrator). Marie Bender Guel (Interested Party/First Counsin of Decendent) in pro per for her "Aunt and Counsin's Estate". Veronica Chico (Interested Party/First ) in pro per.

Ms. Bender-Guel stated that she is the first cousin of the decedent. Veronica Chico stated that she was here today for her Mother, Anita Rosas, who is also a relative of the decedent. Mr. Weinkauf requested a 30 day continuance to file the final account, stated that the decedent's son did not do the federal 706 when his mother passed away, that the estate liabilities are over $500,000 which would wipe out the current residue of $304,000, that they are trying to figure out what to do about that situation, that they are talking to an expert estate planner, but that they haven't come up with any good ideas to get around that problem other than to attempt to negotiate. Ms. Bender Guel and Ms. Chico presented their respective concerns for the record. Mr. Weinkauf stated that there 101 heirs in this matter, that there will be a Petition to Determine Entitlement in the decedent's son's estate, and that with regard to this estate that they will just close it out and ask that the Court allow payment towards the federal taxes and interest. The Court continued the matter to October 25, 2011 for the status on the filing of a petition for final distribution; no appearances are required if the petition is on file.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2584-2586 Sutter Street, San Francisco, Ca 94115)
Off Calendar Aug-27-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number 291698 The Estate of Felix R. Panol.

The sale was confirmed at $645,000.00 for 100% interest of the property on the terms set forth in the petition. The Court granted the petitions (subject to the written orders).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
